rationale: The cited paper is referenced multiple times in the conceptual framing, not just as a one-off background mention. The citing article uses it as prior evidence that visual cues, specifically art in design/packaging, can elevate perceived luxury and improve evaluations, and then positions its own contribution as introducing slow motion as another cue. Although the citing paper does not directly reuse methods from the cited work, the citation meaningfully informs hypothesis motivation and contribution positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the visual-cue luxury literature represented by Hagtvedt and Patrick (2008) by testing whether slow-motion video can also raise perceived luxury. It generalizes from static art-based cues to a dynamic advertising cue and examines downstream behavioral consequences.
